description:
You are a security auditor who has been asked to provide a quick, human-readable summary of the different UNIX permission strings that occur in a raw inventory file.  
  1. The raw inventory already exists at  
     /home/user/permission_audit/raw_permissions.txt  
     and consists of one permission string per line (e.g. “rwxr-xr-x”).  
  2. Produce a frequency report, using nothing more complex than a single pipeline that employs the classic *sort* and *uniq* utilities (with the “count” option).  
  3. Redirect the final, **sorted-by-frequency-descending** result into a new file named  
     /home/user/permission_audit/permission_frequency.log  
  4. The format **must** exactly match what the traditional command sequence  
     “sort raw_permissions.txt | uniq -c | sort -nr”  
     would emit.  That is:  
     • every line contains the count right-aligned in a 7-character field (so single-digit counts have six leading spaces, two-digit counts five, etc.);  
     • a single space follows the count field;  
     • then the permission string;  
     • no extra spaces or trailing whitespace;  
     • lines are separated by UNIX new-lines (LF).  
     
     Example of the required layout (spacing is significant):
[6 spaces]8 rwxr-xr-x
[6 spaces]7 rw-r--r--
[6 spaces]3 rwxr-xr--
[6 spaces]2 rwx------

  5. Do NOT change the contents of /home/user/permission_audit/raw_permissions.txt.  Only create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the report file mentioned in step 3.  
  6. When finished, you should be able to view the report with  
        cat /home/user/permission_audit/permission_frequency.log  
     and see the correctly sorted, properly spaced four-line output.  
     
  (Tip: a single command pipeline with two uses of “sort” and one use of “uniq -c” is enough.)
truth:
Existing file before the task starts:
    Path: /home/user/permission_audit/raw_permissions.txt  
    Contents (20 lines, in this exact order):
rwxr-xr-x
rw-r--r--
rwxr-xr-x
rw-r--r--
rw-r--r--
rwx------
rwxr-xr-x
rwx------
rwxr-xr-x
rw-r--r--
rwxr-xr-x
rwxr-xr--
rwxr-xr--
rw-r--r--
rwxr-xr--
rwxr-xr-x
rw-r--r--
rw-r--r--
rwxr-xr-x
rwxr-xr-x

  File that must be created by the agent:
    Path: /home/user/permission_audit/permission_frequency.log  
    Exact contents (each “·” below represents a literal space character):
·······8·rwxr-xr-x
·······7·rw-r--r--
·······3·rwxr-xr--
·······2·rwx------

  (There are six leading space characters before each single-digit count, so the count occupies a 7-character field.)
